vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but incomplete. P3 (Pothos with rabbit) and P1 (String of Nickels) are present. The yellow pot (P1) is visible but appears empty of foliage. The black pot (P3) contains the rabbit and a single leaf. The black pot (P4/P2) contains a single, struggling sprout. There is a significant discrepancy between the registry and the visual evidence regarding the health and presence of the plants.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot appears empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Mexican Mint)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: New Introduction/Intervention (Single sprout with eggshell debris)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot, no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P2: No evidence of Mexican Mint; pot appears to be part of the empty soil mass.
plant_audit.P3: Single Pothos leaf remains; rabbit anchor is stable.
plant_audit.P4: Single, pale, elongated sprout; appears to be a new, struggling seedling.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P4, likely used as a calcium supplement or soil amendment.

temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-4: P1/P2 area shows slight soil settling.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-3: Introduction of a white cable/wire near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_t-2: Cable remains; P4 sprout shows slight elongation.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: P4 sprout appears slightly more chlorotic (pale).

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. P1 and P2 are effectively lost. P3 is surviving on a single leaf. P4 is a fragile, potentially etiolated seedling struggling in low-light conditions. The use of eggshells suggests an attempt at intervention, but the lack of foliage indicates a failure to thrive.
anomalies: White cable intrusion near yellow pot
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is failing. The registry's expected plants (P1, P2) are absent or dead. The Pothos (P3) is reduced to a single leaf, and the new sprout (P4) is showing signs of light starvation (etiolation). The presence of eggshells indicates an attempt to provide nutrients, but the overall state is one of critical neglect or environmental mismatch.
